perturbation.original_text: Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include (1) standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, and for such purpose Netzee will use commercially reasonable efforts to make and include (without limitation) as such modifications changes and upgrades necessary to comply with generally applicable industry and regulatory requirements of which it is notified (2) 5x12 call-in support between hours of 6:30 a.m. and 6:30 p.m. Eastern Time Monday through Friday, (3) in the event of material discrepancies between the Services as provided and the specifications for such Services (which shall be furnished in reasonably acceptable form), reasonable effort (in relation to importance, impact, and scheduled development plans) to correct those discrepancies, and (4) availability of basic Services with at least 99% uptime between 4:00 a.m Monday and 7:00 p.m. Friday and between 8:00 a.m. Saturday and 2:00 p.m. Saturday Eastern Time, exclusive of outages caused by off-peak prescheduled maintenance or causes beyond Netzee's reasonable control (including third-party equipment or communications).
perturbation.changed_text: Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally. Netzee will determine the extent of support and modifications based on its internal assessment.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific inclusions of maintenance such as 5x12 call-in support, discrepancy correction, and uptime guarantees while simultaneously referencing 'standard maintenance upgrades', creates an in-text contradiction regarding what level of maintenance is provided. The original mentions it, but the new revision is omited. The removed clauses define clear maintenance obligations, so the modified revision implies a discretionary approach to maintenance obligations, contradicting the initial detailed service agreement. The final sentence contradicts the clear 99% uptime mentioned, making the whole section vague and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally. Netzee will determine the extent of support and modifications based on its internal assessment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes specific maintenance obligations (5x12 support, discrepancy correction, uptime guarantees) and replaces them with a vague statement that Netzee will determine the extent of support based on internal assessment. This directly contradicts the implied detailed service agreement in the original text that included concrete support levels and service guarantees, rendering the agreement vague and unenforceable. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location, the change directly creates a contradiction with the original intent of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT IS GOVERNED BY, AND SHALL BE SUBJECT TO, THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT BETWEEN NETZEE AND BANKERS BANK, INCLUDING THE LIMITED WARRANTY AND DISCLAIMER AND LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY PROVIDED THEREIN. SUCH TERMS AND CONDITIONS ARE HEREBY AFFIRMED BY BOTH PARTIES AND INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE IN THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, ALL LICENSES GRANTED OR EXERCISABLE THEREUNDER.
perturbation.changed_text: THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT STANDS INDEPENDENTLY, AND IS NOT SUBJECT TO THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT BETWEEN NETZEE AND BANKERS BANK.
perturbation.explanation: The original text makes the Maintenance Agreement subject to the Master Agreement, including warranty and liability clauses. Changing this to state that the Maintenance Agreement is independent contradicts the original incorporation by reference. This contradicts the Master Agreement's warranty terms, which would otherwise apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT STANDS INDEPENDENTLY, AND IS NOT SUBJECT TO THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT BETWEEN NETZEE AND BANKERS BANK.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly incorporates the Master Agreement's terms into the Maintenance Agreement. The changed text states that the Maintenance Agreement stands independently and is not subject to the Master Agreement. This directly contradicts the incorporation by reference, creating a clear conflict within the document and is an example of Omissions – In Text Contradiction.
